name: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina (Federacija Bosne i Hercegovine / FBiH)
summary: The Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina (Bosnian/Croatian: Federacija Bosne i Hercegovine / FBiH) is a Balkan civil-law constitutionally-recognised entity of Bosnia and Herzegovina (one of two constitutionally-recognised entities under the Dayton Accord 1995 framework: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina + Republika Srpska, with Brčko District operating as condominium third entity) — structurally distinctive globally as the only sub-state-level entity constituted via formal pre-Dayton-Accord-1995 Washington Agreement framework (Washington Agreement 18 March 1994 — formal peace agreement between Bosnia and Herzegovina + Republic of Croatia + Bosnian Croats ending Croat-Bosniak War 1992-1994 within Bosnian War 1992-1995 + establishing Bosniak-Croat federation framework, distinct from rest-of-Bosnia-and-Herzegovina entities where Dayton Accord 1995 is sole establishing framework), as the only sub-state-level entity operating formal dual-ethnic-constituent-peoples governance framework (formal constitutional framework requiring Bosniak + Croat + Other constituent-peoples representation at executive + legislative + judicial branches — distinct from rest-of-sub-state-level entities operating mono-ethnic governance frameworks), as the only sub-state-level entity operating formal 10-cantons sub-sub-territorial framework (Federation comprises 10 cantons each with own constitution + government + parliament + judiciary — Una-Sana + Posavina + Tuzla + Zenica-Doboj + Bosnian Podrinje + Central Bosnia + Herzegovina-Neretva + West Herzegovina + Sarajevo + Canton 10, distinct from rest-of-sub-state-level entities operating unitary or simple sub-territorial frameworks), and as the central jurisdiction of one of the most distinctive modern post-Cold-War European federalisation frameworks (1994-1995 Bosniak-Croat federation framework + 1995 Dayton Accord federalisation framework + 2002 Constituent Peoples Decision Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina Article U-5/98 — formalising Bosniak + Croat + Serb constituent-peoples framework across Federation + Republika Srpska entities). The Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina territory historically operated under Ottoman 1463-1878 + Austro-Hungarian 1878-1918 + Kingdom of Yugoslavia 1918-1941 + WWII Independent State of Croatia 1941-1945 + Socialist Federal Republic of Yugoslavia 1945-1992 + Bosnian War period 1992-1995 + Washington Agreement 1994+ frameworks. Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina comprises ~26,110 km² with a population of ~2.2 million. Family-law framework operates under a dual federal Bosnia and Herzegovina civil-law framework + Federation-specific Family Law of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina 2005 framework. The Supreme Court of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina is the apex regional court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Bosnia and Herzegovina is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory and party (acceded 23 August 1991 effective 1 December 1991 via succession from SFRY) — Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina is governed by Bosnia and Herzegovina Hague-Convention-1980 framework.

statutory_framework.title: Washington Agreement
statutory_framework.relevance: Washington Agreement signed 18 March 1994 — formal peace agreement between Bosnia and Herzegovina + Republic of Croatia + Bosnian Croats ending Croat-Bosniak War 1992-1994 + establishing Bosniak-Croat federation framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Dayton Accord 1995 (Annex IV: Bosnia and Herzegovina Constitution)
statutory_framework.title: Dayton Accord Annex IV — BiH Constitution
statutory_framework.relevance: Dayton Accord signed Paris 14 December 1995 (formally General Framework Agreement for Peace in Bosnia and Herzegovina) — Annex IV containing Bosnia and Herzegovina Constitution establishing Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina + Republika Srpska entity framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina 1994 (amended)
statutory_framework.title: Constitution of the Federation of BiH
statutory_framework.relevance: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina Constitution adopted 30 March 1994 + multiple amendments post-Dayton Accord 1995 — entity-level constitutional framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Family Law of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina 2005
statutory_framework.title: Family Law of Federation BiH
statutory_framework.relevance: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina-specific Family Law of 2005 — entity-level family-law framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Constituent Peoples Decision U-5/98 (Constitutional Court of BiH)
statutory_framework.title: Constituent Peoples Decision U-5/98
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina U-5/98 Partial Decision of 1 July 2000 — formalising Bosniak + Croat + Serb constituent-peoples framework across Federation + Republika Srpska entities.

statutory_framework.citation: Hague Convention 1980 — Bosnia and Herzegovina accession 23 August 1991 via SFRY succession
statutory_framework.title: Hague Convention 1980 — Bosnia and Herzegovina
statutory_framework.relevance: Bosnia and Herzegovina Hague Convention 1980 accession 23 August 1991 effective 1 December 1991 via succession from SFRY — applicable to Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina (Vrhovni sud Federacije Bosne i Hercegovine)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina — final constitutional jurisdiction over Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina matters.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina
professional_regulators.role: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina entity-level professional regulatory framework.

anonymisation_convention: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina family-court decisions are anonymised per Bosnia and Herzegovina court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Bosnian War begins + Croat-Bosniak War
key_developments.description: Bosnian War 6 April 1992 - 14 December 1995 + Croat-Bosniak War 19 June 1992 - 23 February 1994 — armed-conflict-period framework.

key_developments.title: Washington Agreement + Federation established
key_developments.description: Washington Agreement signed 18 March 1994 — formal peace agreement between Bosnia and Herzegovina + Republic of Croatia + Bosnian Croats ending Croat-Bosniak War + establishing Bosniak-Croat federation framework + Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina Constitution adopted 30 March 1994.

key_developments.title: Dayton Accord + Federation formalisation
key_developments.description: Dayton Accord signed Paris 14 December 1995 — formal peace agreement ending Bosnian War + formal constitutional recognition of Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina + Republika Srpska entity framework.

key_developments.title: Constituent Peoples Decision U-5/98
key_developments.description: Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina U-5/98 Partial Decision of 1 July 2000 — formalising Bosniak + Croat + Serb constituent-peoples framework across Federation + Republika Srpska entities.

key_developments.title: Family Law of Federation BiH
key_developments.description: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina-specific Family Law of 2005 — entity-level family-law framework.

structural_findings: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina operates a civil-law constitutionally-recognised entity framework — places Federation of BiH in the Balkan Bosnia and Herzegovina entity cluster.

Only sub-state-level entity constituted via formal pre-Dayton-Accord-1995 Washington Agreement framework is structurally distinctive globally — Washington Agreement 18 March 1994 ending Croat-Bosniak War 1992-1994 + establishing Bosniak-Croat federation framework.

Only sub-state-level entity operating formal dual-ethnic-constituent-peoples governance framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal constitutional Bosniak + Croat + Other representation at executive + legislative + judicial branches.

Only sub-state-level entity operating formal 10-cantons sub-sub-territorial framework is structurally distinctive globally — 10 cantons each with own constitution + government + parliament + judiciary.

Central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern post-Cold-War European federalisation frameworks is structurally distinctive globally — 1994-1995 federation establishment + 2002 Constituent Peoples Decision framework.
Washington Agreement 1994 + Dayton Accord 1995 + Constituent Peoples Decision 2000 federalisation trajectory is structurally distinctive globally.
10-cantons sub-sub-territorial framework with own constitutional frameworks is structurally distinctive within Balkan cluster.
Bosnia and Herzegovina Hague-Convention-1980-accession-1991 framework applies via SFRY succession.

sources.title: Government of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina (Vlada Federacije BiH)
sources.publisher: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina Government

sources.title: OSCE Mission to Bosnia and Herzegovina

editorial_notes: Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina jurisdiction sidecar — civil-law Balkan Bosnia and Herzegovina constitutionally-recognised entity (Washington Agreement 1994 + Dayton Accord 1995 Annex IV BiH Constitution + Constitution of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina 1994 + Family Law of Federation BiH 2005 + Constituent Peoples Decision U-5/98 + 10 cantons sub-sub-territorial framework + Bosnia and Herzegovina Hague-1980 via SFRY succession). Only sub-state-level entity constituted via formal pre-Dayton-Accord-1995 Washington Agreement framework globally + only sub-state-level entity operating formal dual-ethnic-constituent-peoples governance framework globally + only sub-state-level entity operating formal 10-cantons sub-sub-territorial framework globally + central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern post-Cold-War European federalisation frameworks + Washington-Dayton-Constituent-Peoples federalisation trajectory.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Balkan + civil-law + Bosnia-and-Herzegovina-entity cluster + Washington-Agreement-1994-globally-distinctive + Dayton-Accord-1995-Annex-IV-BiH-Constitution + Bosniak-Croat-dual-ethnic-constituent-peoples-governance + 10-cantons-sub-sub-territorial-framework + Constituent-Peoples-Decision-U-5-98-2000 + Bosnia-and-Herzegovina-Hague-1980-1991 clusters within the corpus.
